title: NSC and IEB 2025 results impress
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"KNYSNA NEWS - Matriculants across Knysna experienced a mix of anxiety, relief and excitement this week as the class of 2025 received their final school results.","html":"KNYSNA NEWS - Matriculants across Knysna experienced a mix of anxiety, relief and excitement this week as the class of 2025 received their final school results."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Independent Examinations Board (IEB) candidates received their results on Monday 12 January, while National Senior Certificate (NSC) learners accessed their marks on Tuesday 13 January, marking the official end of a long and demanding school journey."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Local schools reported a generally positive atmosphere as pupils processed their outcomes, with many celebrating passes that will open doors to tertiary education, learnerships and the workplace.","html":"Local schools reported a generally positive atmosphere as pupils processed their outcomes, with many celebrating passes that will open doors to tertiary education, learnerships and the workplace."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"NSC learners and their families gathered at schools across Knysna to collect results on Tuesday. Knysna High School achieved a 100% pass rate, while Concordia High School recorded an 85.5% pass rate.","html":"NSC learners and their families gathered at schools across Knysna to collect results on Tuesday. Knysna High School achieved a 100% pass rate, while Concordia High School recorded an 85.5% pass rate."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Improvements were also noted at Knysna Secondary School and Percy Mdala High School. Knysna Secondary School increased its pass rate from 86.2% in 2024 to 87.2% in 2025, while Percy Mdala High School improved from 92.5% in 2024 to 94.9% this year."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Nationally, the IEB recorded an overall pass rate of 98.31%, with 89.12% of candidates achieving bachelor's passes. Locally, Oakhill School reflected this strong performance with a 100% pass rate.","html":"Nationally, the IEB recorded an overall pass rate of 98.31%, with 89.12% of candidates achieving bachelor's passes. Locally, Oakhill School reflected this strong performance with a 100% pass rate."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"According to the Department of Basic Education, the class of 2025 achieved 13.5% higher certificate passes, 28.1% diploma passes and 46.6% bachelor's passes nationally. The Western Cape placed fifth among the provinces with an overall pass rate of 88.20%.","html":"According to the Department of Basic Education, the class of 2025 achieved 13.5% higher certificate passes, 28.1% diploma passes and 46.6% bachelor's passes nationally. The Western Cape placed fifth among the provinces with an overall pass rate of 88.20%."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"As Knysna's class of 2025 closes this chapter, attention now turns to what lies ahead."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"For many, this moment marks not an ending, but the beginning of a hopeful new journey.","html":"For many, this moment marks not an ending, but the beginning of a hopeful new journey."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Knysna Secondary School’s hall was abuzz as matriculants of 2025 gathered to receive their final results. Here are Caylin Jantjies, Ashwill Bosman and Fayghen Manuel with their results. Photo: Kyla Laing","html":"Knysna Secondary School’s hall was abuzz as matriculants of 2025 gathered to receive their final results. Here are Caylin Jantjies, Ashwill Bosman and Fayghen Manuel with their results. Photo: Kyla Laing"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Matrics of Knysna Secondary School eagerly await their 2025 NSC results. Photo: Kyla Laing","html":"Matrics of Knysna Secondary School eagerly await their 2025 NSC results. Photo: Kyla Laing"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"It’s a pass for both Tesmia Abrahams and Raydene Brown of Knysna Secondary School. Photo: Kyla Laing","html":"It’s a pass for both Tesmia Abrahams and Raydene Brown of Knysna Secondary School. Photo: Kyla Laing"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Matriculants of Knysna Seconday School with a teacher. From left are Leighswin Luiters, teacher A Cloete, Shaunville Talmaggies and Delano Gelant. Photo: Kyla Laing","html":"Matriculants of Knysna Seconday School with a teacher. From left are Leighswin Luiters, teacher A Cloete, Shaunville Talmaggies and Delano Gelant. Photo: Kyla Laing"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
